phrases.intent_en: Ask for a sample at a cheese stall
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me faire goûter ce fromage, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-ryay-VOO muh fair goo-TAY suh froh-MAZH, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Les Halles Paul Bocuse (Les Halles de Lyon) is the city's prestigious covered food market. Vendors are proud of their products and tastings are generally welcome.

phrases.intent_en: Order a quenelle at a counter
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais une quenelle de brochet, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DREH oon kuh-NEL duh broh-SHEH, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: Quenelles de brochet (pike dumplings in cream sauce) are Lyon's most iconic dish. The market is one of the best places in the city to try them.

responses.phonetic: lah kuh-NEL duh broh-SHEH, seh PREH
responses.french: La quenelle de brochet, c'est prêt.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which charcuterie is most typically Lyonnais
phrases.text_local: Quelle charcuterie est la plus typiquement lyonnaise ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel shar-koo-TREE eh lah ploo tee-peek-MAHN lyoh-NEZ
phrases.cultural_note: Lyon is France's charcuterie capital. Look for rosette de Lyon (dry salami), jésus (cured sausage), and grattons (fried pork crackling) — all local specialties.

responses.english: The dry sausage or the rosette.
responses.phonetic: luh soh-see-SOHN SEK oo lah roh-ZET
responses.french: Le saucisson sec ou la rosette.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a praline tart to take away
phrases.text_local: Avez-vous une tarte à la praline à emporter ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah-VAY-voo oon TART ah lah prah-LEEN ah ahm-por-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: The tarte à la praline — a bright pink tart made with caramelised almonds — is Lyon's signature pastry. It is intensely sweet and utterly unmistakable.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market opens in the morning
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ouvre le marché le matin ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR OOV-ruh luh mar-SHAY luh mah-TEHN
phrases.cultural_note: The market opens Tuesday through Sunday, early morning. It is at its liveliest mid-morning. Many stalls close by lunchtime on weekdays.
